description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline with a build-meltano stage that builds Docker images for Meltano using the Dockerfile at docker/prod/Dockerfile. Define a hidden .build_prod job template that runs before_script steps: source .gitlab/ci/scripts/docker_build_before_script.sh, source .gitlab/ci/scripts/python_image_version.sh, export EXTRA_ARGS="--build-arg BASE_IMAGE=$CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE/base:$BASE_SHA-python$PYTHON_IMAGE_VERSION", and export IMAGE_TAG=$CI_COMMIT_SHA-python$PYTHON_IMAGE_VERSION; it extends .docker_build and .parallel:python_version, optionally needs meltano_base and meltano_base_forks, sets DOCKERFILE and IMAGE_NAME variables, and stores the built image in $CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE while archiving the dist directory as an artifact. Add a meltano job that extends .build_prod and .only:version-tags, runs .gitlab/ci/scripts/docker_build_script.sh, tags the image as $CI_COMMIT_TAG-python$PYTHON_IMAGE_VERSION and latest-python$PYTHON_IMAGE_VERSION, pushes both tags, and if PYTHON_VERSION is 3.8 also tags and pushes $CI_COMMIT_TAG and latest. Add a meltano_dev job that extends .build_prod, runs on all branches except master in the meltano/meltano project. Add a meltano_dev_forks job that extends .build_prod, runs on all branches and tags except those belonging to meltano/meltano. Add a meltano_edge job that extends .build_prod and .only:master, runs the docker_build_script.sh, tags and pushes edge-python$PYTHON_IMAGE_VERSION, and if PYTHON_VERSION is 3.8 also tags and pushes edge. All jobs produce the dist artifact.
